tracks.title: Airport Boarding Announcement
tracks.description: A typical flight boarding announcement at an international airport.

segments.text: Good afternoon, passengers.
This is the final boarding call for Vietnam Airlines flight VN310 to Hanoi.
All remaining passengers, please proceed immediately to gate 14.
The boarding doors will close in five minutes.
Please have your boarding pass and identification ready for inspection.
If you are traveling with small children or require special assistance, our crew will be happy to help you.
We thank you for choosing Vietnam Airlines and wish you a pleasant flight.

vocabulary.example: All passengers must show their boarding pass.

quiz.question: Which flight number is being called?
quiz.explanation: The announcement says 'flight VN310 to Hanoi'.

quiz.question: Which gate should passengers go to?
quiz.explanation: The announcement says 'please proceed immediately to gate 14'.

quiz.question: What two documents should passengers have ready?
quiz.explanation: 'Please have your boarding pass and identification ready for inspection.'

quiz.question: Who will the crew help, according to the announcement?
options: Passengers traveling with small children or needing special assistance
No one — the announcement does not say
quiz.explanation: 'If you are traveling with small children or require special assistance, our crew will be happy to help you.'

tracks.title: Morning News Brief: Vietnam's Tech Sector
tracks.description: A short news segment about Vietnam's growing technology industry.

segments.text: Good morning. Here are today's top stories.
Vietnam's technology sector continues to attract foreign investment, with several major companies opening new offices in Ho Chi Minh City this month.
According to a report released yesterday, the country now has more than two thousand registered tech startups, double the number from five years ago.
Most of these companies focus on financial services, e-commerce, and digital media.
Government officials say that supportive policies and a young workforce are the main reasons for this growth.
However, experts also warn that infrastructure and education must keep pace with demand if the sector is to remain competitive.
In other news, the weather in northern Vietnam will remain cool with light rain through the weekend.
Stay with us for more updates after the break.

vocabulary.example: The technology sector is growing fast.
She works at a tech startup.

quiz.question: How many tech startups does the report mention?
quiz.explanation: 'The country now has more than two thousand registered tech startups.'

quiz.question: How has the number of startups changed?
options: It is the same as five years ago
It has doubled in five years
It has tripled in five years
quiz.explanation: 'Double the number from five years ago.'

quiz.question: Which sectors do most startups focus on?
quiz.explanation: 'Most of these companies focus on financial services, e-commerce, and digital media.'

quiz.question: What do experts warn about?
options: Infrastructure and education must keep pace with demand
quiz.explanation: 'Experts also warn that infrastructure and education must keep pace with demand.'

quiz.question: What will the weather be like in northern Vietnam?
quiz.explanation: 'The weather in northern Vietnam will remain cool with light rain through the weekend.'

tracks.title: Ordering at a Coffee Shop
tracks.description: A short dialogue between a customer and a barista.

segments.speaker: Barista
segments.text: Hi there, what can I get for you today?

segments.speaker: Customer
segments.text: Hi. Can I have a medium iced coffee, please?

segments.speaker: Barista
segments.text: Sure. Would you like it with milk or sugar?

segments.speaker: Customer
segments.text: A little milk, please. No sugar.

segments.speaker: Barista
segments.text: Anything to eat with that?

segments.speaker: Customer
segments.text: Could I also get a chocolate croissant?

segments.speaker: Barista
segments.text: Of course. That comes to seventy-five thousand dong. Will that be for here or to go?

segments.speaker: Customer
segments.text: For here, please.

segments.speaker: Barista
segments.text: Great. I'll bring it to your table in a few minutes.

vocabulary.example: Is that for here or to go?

quiz.question: What did the customer order to drink?
quiz.explanation: The customer says 'Can I have a medium iced coffee, please?'

quiz.question: How does the customer take their coffee?
options: With lots of sugar, no milk

quiz.question: What food did the customer order?
quiz.explanation: 'Could I also get a chocolate croissant?'

quiz.question: Is the order for here or to go?

tracks.title: Podcast: The Science of Building Habits
tracks.description: A short podcast monologue about how habits form.

segments.text: Welcome back to The Quiet Edge, the podcast about small changes that lead to large results.
In this episode, we explore the science of habits — why some behaviors stick easily and others fade away within days.
Researchers describe habits as automatic responses to recurring situations.
When you wake up in the morning and reach for your phone without thinking, that is a habit in action.
The brain is using a shortcut to save energy, instead of consciously deciding what to do.
Three elements typically work together: a cue, a routine, and a reward.
The cue is what triggers the behavior — for example, the sound of an alarm.
The routine is what you actually do — reaching for the phone.
The reward is what your brain receives — perhaps the small pleasure of seeing messages or news.
Understanding this loop is the first step to changing it.
To build a new habit, you do not need motivation — you need a small, reliable cue and an immediate reward.
To break an old one, you keep the cue and reward but change the routine in between.
We will go deeper into specific techniques in next week's episode.
Until then, take care, and keep your changes small.

vocabulary.example: This new habit stuck after three weeks.
A cue can be a place, time, or sound.
The alarm triggers my morning routine.
Use a reliable cue every day.

quiz.question: How do researchers describe habits?
quiz.explanation: 'Researchers describe habits as automatic responses to recurring situations.'

quiz.question: What three elements form a habit loop?
quiz.explanation: 'Three elements typically work together: a cue, a routine, and a reward.'

quiz.question: What does the speaker say you need to build a new habit?
options: A small, reliable cue and an immediate reward
A big punishment if you fail
quiz.explanation: 'You do not need motivation — you need a small, reliable cue and an immediate reward.'

quiz.question: How do you break an old habit, according to the podcast?
options: Change everything — cue, routine, and reward
Keep the cue and reward, change the routine in between
quiz.explanation: 'Keep the cue and reward but change the routine in between.'

quiz.question: What is the speaker's closing advice?
options: Take care, and keep your changes small
Try a hundred new habits at once
quiz.explanation: 'Take care, and keep your changes small.'
